Pike in the #GreatLakes spawn right after ice melts, typically March/April. They migrate into shallow wetlands + broadcast their eggs over aquatic plants. Newly hatched babies have a sticky gland on their head that allows them to attach to vegetation until they absorb their yolk sac #25DaysofFishmas

Steelhead/rainbow trout are native to North America west of the Rockies, but have been introduced for food and sport in at least 45 countries + every continent except Antarctica. They’ve been introduced to the #GreatLakes to provide fishing opportunities since the late 1800s #25DaysofFishmas

The spotted gar has a wide but somewhat disjointed distribution in central North America stretching across 18 states and Ontario. The southern #GreatLakes (southern Lake Michigan, southern Lake Huron, and Erie) are pretty much as far north as they can be found #25DaysofFishmas
Lake whitefish are spawning this time of year, so commercial season is closed. The eggs will overwinter and then hatch early in the spring. #GreatLakes

Uroplatus timhortonicus, or the Canadian Leaf Tailed Gecko is a rare species of gecko only found on around the shores of Lake Ontario. Noted for it's extreme friendliness, it's vocalizations sound suspiciously like "Sorry" and "Eh?". Becomes defensive if asked about auto insurance.

Want an easy way to start a fight? Say “Great Lakes steelhead” to a Western angler! There’s vigorous debate among anglers as to whether migratory rainbow trout in the #GreatLakes should be considered “true” steelhead because they don’t go to the ocean (though look/act similarly) #25DaysofFishmas

Kiyi is endemic to (meaning only found in) the #GreatLakes and was historically widespread in the deep waters of all except Erie. It’s currently considered extant only in .bsky.social and was last recorded in Lakes Ontario in 1964, Huron in 1973, and Michigan in 1974 #25DaysofFishmas

Here's another #flyfishing location: Wisconsin's Bois Brule River. I've caught some resident trout there, but my attempts to be there when the steelhead from Lake Superior are running always seem to get the timing wrong. I'll keep trying and someday I'll meet those steelhead.

🌊 #Invasive species are a pressing concern in the #GreatLakes region; the Grass carp being one. Prolific eaters, they're capable of devastating wetland environments and upending the commercial #fishing industry. Stay up to speed on what's being done to keep them out: thenarwhal.ca/ontario-gras... 🎣
As a #GreatLakes wetland resident species, it is always fun to find them min the nets along with the #GarWeek gars, juvenile fishes (perch, sunfish, bass, etc), and northern pike.

Pygmy whitefish aren’t targeted by anglers or commercial fishing, but are prey for predatory #GreatLakes fishes like lake trout. In a twist, the recovery of lake trout in Lake Superior in recent years may contribute (in part) to lower numbers of pygmy whitefish seen since the 1990s #25DaysofFishmas

Check out this new paper led by .bsky.social on recruitment trends in coregonines dating back to the 1960s in the #GreatLakes and #LakeSimcoe. Lake whitefish showed synchrony across lakes whereas cisco were more sporadic. academic.oup.com/icesjms/adva...

There are 3 species of bullhead catfish in the #GreatLakes: black, brown, and yellow. Yellow is easier to distinguish because of its light-colored barbels (whiskers), but brown/black can be tricky. Black bullheads are generally uniform in color while browns are often more mottled #25DaysofFishmas

No, you’re not seeing double - while steelhead and rainbow trout are the same species (Oncorhynchus mykiss), steelhead are anadromous (born in rivers but migrate to either the ocean or #GreatLakes as adults), while rainbows are homebodies who stay in freshwater (stream residents) #25DaysofFishmas

Unlike other Pacific salmon introduced to the #GreatLakes (Coho and Chinook), steelhead don’t automatically die after spawning once. Those survivors are called “kelts,” and while they can spawn multiple times, they often only spawn again once or twice (it takes a lot of energy!) #25DaysofFishmas

Muskellunge - commonly referred to as Muskie is a member of the Pikes family. It is native to St. Lawrence River - Great Lakes, Hudson Bay (Red River), and Mississippi River basins in North America. It can reach a maximum length of 183 cm (6 feet). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/8351...

Yellow Perch - is a member of the perches family. It is native to Atlantic, Arctic, Great Lakes, and Mississippi River basins from Nova Scotia to Mackenzie drainage, Northwest Territories in Canada, and south to Illinois and Nebraska. It can reach a maximum length of 50 cm (19.7 inches).

Walleye - it is a member of the Perches family. It is native to the Great Lakes, Arctic, and Mississippi River basins from Quebec to Northwest Territories in Canada, and south to Alabama and Arkansas in the USA. It can reach max length of 107 cm (42.1 inches). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/4518...

Orangespotted Sunfish - is a member of the Sunfish family. It is native to lower Great Lakes, Hudson Bay, and Mississippi River basin from Ohio to southern North Dakota, and south to Louisiana. Its maximum length is 17.5 cm (6.9 in). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/8351...

Longear Sunfish - It is a member of the Sunfish family. It is native to North America from northeastern Mexico and north to the Great Lakes. It can reach a maximum length of 24 cm (9.4 inches). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/8351...

Warmouth - is a member of the Sunfish family. It is native to Great Lakes and Mississippi River basins from western Pennsylvania to Minnesota in the USA, and south to the Gulf of Mexico. It can reach a maximum length of 31 cm (12.2 inches).

Bluegill Sunfish - is a member of the Sunfish family and is native to the St. Lawrence - Great Lakes and Mississippi river basin; Quebec to northern Mexico. It can reach a maximum length of 41 cm (16.1 inches) and a maximum published weight of 2.2 kg (4.85 pounds). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/8338...

Smallmouth Bass - is a member of the sunfish family and it is native to the St Lawrence -Great Lakes system, Hudson, and Mississippi river systems. It can reach a maximum length of 69cm (about 27 inches) and a maximum published weight of 5.4 kg (12 pounds). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/3033...

Largemouth Black Bass - member of the sunfish family. It is native to the Great Lakes, and Mississippi River basins from southern Quebec to Minnesota and south to Gulf of Mexico They can reach a max length of 99 cm (39 inches). www.redbubble.com/shop/ap/3245...
